trajectory_summary: The call opened with a customer who had already mentally closed the file, and the rep never found a lever to reopen it. The one substantive pitch attempt was a generic feature statement delivered after two explicit exits, with no tie to anything James had actually said. The call ended where it started — James disengaged, rep accepting the outcome.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is listed as unknown. Based on the transcript, the outcome is a clean non-conversion with no appointment booked or tentative. The call is not a loss from a previously warm state — it was never warm. The more precise read is: the rep failed to move a cold, self-disqualified customer off his stated conclusion, and the call ended without any forward commitment.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Pitch specificity — use the customer's own words, not a generic feature statement
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Gotcha. Well, I know that our program, it just replaces your current electric bill with a a cheaper solar payment. Right?
primary_coaching_focus.why: James gave the rep three specific hooks across the call: subsidy interest, payback period concern, and a new roof. The rep's only pitch ignored all three and delivered a generic bill-replacement line. On this call, that is the single most repairable gap — the rep had the raw material and did not use it. In the 1-on-1, have the rep rebuild the pitch using only what James said. The goal is not a harder close; it is a more relevant one.

key_moments.quote: Just to make sure, you know, we're on the same page. So you were looking more of, like, just the equipment aspects, not like a full installation or anything like that?
key_moments.stewart_read: The rep's clarifying question confirms James's framing rather than opening a new one. By asking 'just the equipment aspects, not like a full installation?' the rep hands James a clean exit ramp and validates his self-disqualification. Strategic read: the rep is trying to understand the objection before addressing it. Reactive read: the rep is inadvertently co-authoring the customer's reason to leave. The reactive read holds — there is no subsequent move that uses this information to re-engage.

key_moments.quote: I think the the leading ad that I saw showed that there were still subsidies available for solar installations.
key_moments.stewart_read: James volunteered his original motivation unprompted: he saw an ad about subsidies and wanted to understand the cost. This is a genuine interest signal buried inside a disengagement speech. The rep did not anchor on it. A customer who came in asking about subsidies and cost is not a dead lead — he is a customer whose curiosity was never properly engaged.

key_moments.quote: I really don't need solar panels. My electricity usage is relatively small, and payback period is probably too long.
key_moments.stewart_read: James has done his own payback math and concluded solar doesn't pencil. This is a knowledge gap masquerading as a settled decision — he is estimating payback without knowing Ion's actual numbers, current incentive structures, or what 'relatively small' usage looks like against a solar offset. The rep did not challenge the math or create curiosity about whether his estimate is accurate. The flip was available: 'Most people who say that are surprised when they see the actual design — the payback period depends on factors you can't calculate without the system design. That's what the appointment is for.'

key_moments.quote: It's, seasonal. You know? So, but it's it's relatively small. It probably averages a little less than 100 a month probably for year round.
key_moments.stewart_read: James uses 'probably' twice in the same sentence when estimating his own bill. This is not a confident self-disqualification — it is an estimate with visible uncertainty. The rep captured the number and moved immediately to a generic pitch. The bill was not flipped. The double 'probably' was not used as a door. A customer who is uncertain about his own bill is a customer who has not actually done the math the rep could do for him.
